paper.title: The high velocity stars in the Local Stellar Halo from Gaia and LAMOST

review.summary: Paper cross-matches Gaia DR1 (TGAS) astrometry with LAMOST DR4 spectroscopy (~230,000 common stars) to study the kinematics and chemistry of local halo stars. From 436 kinematically-identified halo stars (|V-V_LSR| > 220 km/s), 16 high-velocity (HiVel) stars are selected with V_gsr > 300 km/s. Chemical abundances ([Fe/H], [α/Fe]) and backwards orbit integrations in a Galactic potential are used to investigate origins. Three internal tables: (1) Table 1 — reference population parameters (observed fractions, velocity dispersions σ_U/σ_V/σ_W, asymmetric drift) for thin/thick disk and halo from Bensby+2003; (2) Table 2 — primary catalog of 16 HiVel stars with Gaia DR1 source-id, Galactic coordinates, proper motions, heliocentric radial velocities, atmospheric parameters (Teff, log g, [Fe/H], [α/Fe]); (3) Table 3 — derived Galactocentric Cartesian positions (x, y, z), velocity components (U, V, W), Galactocentric rest-frame velocity V_gsr, heliocentric distance D_sun, orbital eccentricity e, and maximum height above the plane Z_max for the 16 HiVel stars. No machine-readable data tables are provided in the arXiv source. The data are drawn from public surveys (Gaia DR1/TGAS and LAMOST DR4). External resource URLs in the acknowledgments only reference the Gaia and DPAC websites — no CDS/VizieR data links detected.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Reference table reproducing the observed fraction of stars (X), characteristic velocity dispersions (σ_U, σ_V, σ_W), and asymmetric drift (V_asym) for the thin disk, thick disk, and halo populations in the solar neighborhood, from Bensby et al. (2003). Used as input to a Gaussian population model (Eq. 4-5) to estimate thick-disk contamination probability (TD/H) in the kinematically-selected halo sample.

source_refs.caption: Observed fraction of stars for the populations in the solar neighborhood, characteristic velocity dispersions (σ_U, σ_V, and σ_W) and the asymmetric drift (V_asym)
source_refs.label: Table 1

columns.name: Population
columns.meaning: Galactic stellar population: Thin disk (D), Thick disk (TD), or Halo (H).

columns.name: X
columns.meaning: Observed fraction of stars in the solar neighborhood belonging to this population.

columns.name: σ_U
columns.meaning: Characteristic velocity dispersion in the U (radial) direction.

columns.name: σ_V
columns.meaning: Characteristic velocity dispersion in the V (tangential/rotational) direction.

columns.name: σ_W
columns.meaning: Characteristic velocity dispersion in the W (vertical) direction.

columns.name: V_asym
columns.meaning: Asymmetric drift: offset between the mean rotational velocity of the population and the Local Standard of Rest.

internal_tables.evidence: Observed fraction of stars for the populations in the solar neighborhood, characteristic velocity dispersions (σ_U, σ_V, and σ_W) and the asymmetric drift (V_asym)
internal_tables.comments: Values are taken from Bensby et al. (2003, A&A 410, 527). Thin disk: X=0.94, σ=(35,20,16), V_asym=-15. Thick disk: X=0.06, σ=(67,38,35), V_asym=-46. Halo: X=0.0015, σ=(160,90,90), V_asym=-220.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Primary catalog table presenting atmospheric parameters, celestial positions, proper motions, and radial velocities for the 16 high-velocity (HiVel) star candidates selected from the Gaia DR1 + LAMOST DR4 cross-matched sample. This is the core observational dataset of the paper, defining the HiVel sample for subsequent chemical and orbital analysis.

source_refs.caption: Atmospheric parameters and positions for the 16 HiVel stars.
source_refs.label: Table 2

columns.name: Notation
columns.meaning: Identifier for each high-velocity star (HiVel1 through HiVel16), assigned by the authors.

columns.name: source-id
columns.meaning: Gaia DR1 source identifier (TGAS catalog). A unique 64-bit integer identifying the star in the Gaia archive.

columns.name: l
columns.meaning: Galactic longitude from the TGAS catalog.

columns.name: b
columns.meaning: Galactic latitude from the TGAS catalog.

columns.name: μ_α cos(δ)
columns.meaning: Proper motion in right ascension (multiplied by cos(δ)), from Gaia DR1, with uncertainty.

columns.name: μ_δ
columns.meaning: Proper motion in declination, from Gaia DR1, with uncertainty.

columns.name: RV_⊙
columns.meaning: Heliocentric radial velocity measured from LAMOST DR4 spectra (LSP3 pipeline), with uncertainty.

columns.name: T_eff
columns.meaning: Effective temperature from LAMOST LSP3 stellar parameter pipeline (template matching with MILES library).

columns.name: log(g)
columns.meaning: Surface gravity (logarithmic) from LAMOST LSP3 stellar parameter pipeline.

columns.name: [Fe/H]
columns.meaning: Iron abundance relative to solar, from LAMOST LSP3 pipeline, with uncertainty.

columns.name: [α/Fe]
columns.meaning: Overall α-element (Mg, Si, Ca, Ti) to iron abundance ratio, from LAMOST LSP3 pipeline, with uncertainty. Dash indicates no measurement available.

internal_tables.evidence: Atmospheric parameters and positions for the 16 HiVel stars.
internal_tables.comments: Stars HiVel6, HiVel12, and HiVel16 have no [α/Fe] measurement. Three stars (HiVel14, HiVel15, HiVel16) are later found to be unbound based on their very high velocities. The sample was selected from 436 halo stars with V_gsr > 300 km/s, after quality cuts: Teff 3500-8000 K, log g > 0.5, [Fe/H] > -4.0, relative errors in proper motion and distance < 50%.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Derived kinematic and orbital table presenting Galactocentric Cartesian positions (x, y, z), heliocentric distance (D_⊙), Galactocentric velocity components (U, V, W), Galactocentric rest-frame velocity (V_gsr), orbital eccentricity (e), and maximum vertical excursion (Z_max) for the 16 HiVel stars. These quantities are computed from Gaia DR1 astrometry + LAMOST RVs using adopted Solar position/velocity parameters and orbital integration in the McMillan (2017) Galactic potential.

source_refs.caption: Spatial positions and Velocities of 16 HiVel stars

columns.name: Notation
columns.meaning: HiVel star identifier matching Table 2 (HiVel1 through HiVel16).

columns.name: x
columns.meaning: Galactocentric Cartesian X-coordinate (X axis toward Galactic center, l=0°), with uncertainty, computed from TGAS parallax distances using the Astraatmadja & Bailer-Jones (2016) Bayesian distance estimator.

columns.name: y
columns.meaning: Galactocentric Cartesian Y-coordinate (Y axis toward l=90°, direction of Solar motion), with uncertainty.

columns.name: z
columns.meaning: Galactocentric Cartesian Z-coordinate (Z axis toward North Galactic Pole), with uncertainty.

columns.name: U
columns.meaning: Galactocentric radial velocity component (positive outward from GC), with uncertainty. Computed from observed velocities corrected for Solar peculiar motion and LSR.

columns.name: V
columns.meaning: Galactocentric tangential velocity component (positive in direction of Galactic rotation), with uncertainty.

columns.name: W
columns.meaning: Galactocentric vertical velocity component (positive toward North Galactic Pole), with uncertainty.

columns.name: V_gsr
columns.meaning: Total Galactocentric rest-frame speed: magnitude of the 3D space velocity vector in the Galactocentric frame, without uncertainty reported in the table.

columns.name: D_⊙
columns.meaning: Heliocentric distance estimated from TGAS parallax using the Bayesian distance estimator of Astraatmadja & Bailer-Jones (2016), with uncertainty.

columns.name: e
columns.meaning: Orbital eccentricity, defined as e = (r_apo - r_peri)/(r_apo + r_peri), derived from backwards orbit integration in the McMillan (2017) Galactic potential model. Not reported for HiVel14-16 (unbound stars).

columns.name: Z_max
columns.meaning: Maximum vertical height above the Galactic plane reached along the orbit, from backwards integration. Not reported for HiVel14-16 (unbound stars).

internal_tables.evidence: Spatial positions and Velocities of 16 HiVel stars
internal_tables.comments: Three stars (HiVel14, HiVel15, HiVel16) have no orbital parameters (e, Z_max) because they are unbound — their velocities exceed the local escape speed. Adopted parameters: R_⊙=8.2 kpc, V_LSR=220 km/s, solar peculiar motion (10.0, 11.0, 7.0) km/s.

external_resources.description: ESA Gaia mission homepage. The paper uses Gaia DR1 (TGAS) proper motions and parallaxes as primary astrometric input. This URL appears in the acknowledgments as a general resource reference, not a direct data link.

source_refs.context: This work has made use of data from the European Space Agency (ESA) mission Gaia (http://www.cosmos.esa.int/gaia), processed by the Gaia Data Processing and Analysis Consortium (DPAC, http://www.cosmos.esa.int/web/gaia/dpac/consortium).

external_resources.evidence: This work has made use of data from the European Space Agency (ESA) mission Gaia (http://www.cosmos.esa.int/gaia), processed by the Gaia Data Processing and Analysis Consortium (DPAC, http://www.cosmos.esa.int/web/gaia/dpac/consortium).
external_resources.comments: Mission homepage only — the actual data come from Gaia DR1 (TGAS catalog) available at https://gea.esac.esa.int/archive/.

external_resources.description: Gaia Data Processing and Analysis Consortium (DPAC) homepage. Referenced in acknowledgments only.
